sub_pixel_system.notes: Racing game with 3D polygon rendering; sub-pixel precision data not documented

special_mechanics.name: Vehicle Selection and Dynamics
special_mechanics.description: Three selectable vehicles (F-Type: Formula One, Coupe: coupé, 4WD: monster truck) plus unlockable 2WD and bonus Trailer. Each has distinct speed, body, and acceleration characteristics. F-Type has rear-biased center of gravity causing quicker tail drift when cornering.

physics_values.drift_characteristic: F-Type drifts more readily than other vehicles

special_mechanics.name: Speed Boost System
special_mechanics.description: Speed can be boosted during gameplay. Mechanics not specified in documentation.

special_mechanics.name: Turning Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: L and R buttons enable sharper turns.

special_mechanics.name: Collision Damage
special_mechanics.description: Vehicles are damaged by collisions during racing.

special_mechanics.name: Weight-Based Gameplay
special_mechanics.description: Vehicles are intentionally heavier and clumsier than conventional racing games, with this constraint incorporated into level design.

tier_notes: Tier 2: Empirical/qualitative data only. Game is a racing title, not a platformer, so traditional jump/gravity physics do not apply. Focus is on vehicle dynamics and tire-surface interaction. Documentation emphasizes realistic vehicle dynamics but does not provide numerical physics parameters. Development notes indicate designer emphasis on center of gravity, weight distribution, and tire force variation per vehicle.
sources_used: Wikipedia: Stunt Race FX (Gameplay and Development sections)
